items.headline: Indonesia halts nickel ore exports to secure battery supply chain dominance
items.body: Government banned unprocessed nickel shipments Thursday, forcing manufacturers to build refineries locally or lose access to twenty percent of global supply. Move threatens production timelines for electric vehicle makers across three continents who depend on Indonesian ore for batteries.

items.headline: European grain reserves hit fifteen-year low as Black Sea exports remain blocked
items.body: Storage facilities across the continent hold just forty-two days of wheat supply, down from ninety-day average. Bakeries in twelve countries raised prices this week while governments debate releasing strategic stocks originally held for war or famine.

items.headline: Nigeria extends school closures into fourth month as teacher strikes spread nationwide
items.body: Thirty-two million children remain out of class after unions rejected government wage offer covering half of inflation losses. Extended disruption threatens to reverse decade of progress reducing the eight million children already permanently out of school before strikes began.

items.headline: Paraguay activates emergency water rationing as river system drops to century low
items.body: Asunción imposed alternate-day water access for two million residents after Paraguay River fell below navigation and intake thresholds. Drought now affects hydroelectric output across five countries sharing the basin, forcing industries onto diesel generators that triple energy costs.

items.headline: Spain evacuates two hundred troops from Iraq as Middle East conflict forces NATO mission suspension
items.body: Military personnel deployed with coalition and NATO training missions completed emergency withdrawal to Turkey after deteriorating security made operations impossible. Defence minister described complex extraction under active fire with missile exchanges near bases forcing troops into shelters before evacuation flights departed.

items.headline: Government approves five billion euro emergency package slashing fuel taxes as oil crisis hits economy
items.body: Eighty measures including VAT cuts on petrol from twenty-one to ten percent and reduced electricity taxes take effect immediately to shield twenty million households from energy price surge. Package combines immediate relief with expanded renewable energy subsidies but faces uncertain parliamentary approval for housing provisions including nationwide rent freeze.
